tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57973,"title":"[Azure AI Agents Persistent] Docs: CHANGELOG missing entries for TypeSpec emitter migration in 1.2.0-beta.11","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` **Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57631) by `@radhgupta` ## What Changed PR #57631 migrated `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` to the new TypeSpec emitter. The migration entailed: - **Complete regeneration** of all files under `src/Generated/` (dozens of files removed and re-added under the new emitter output) - **New backward-compatibility shims** added under `src/Custom/BackwardCompat/`: - `AbstractTypeConstructors.cs` — constructors for abstract types - `ClientMethodShims.cs` — overloads accepting `IReadOnlyDictionary` metadata (old signature) delegating to the new `IDictionary` signatures on `PersistentAgentsAdministrationClient`, `ThreadMessages`, `ThreadRuns`, `Threads`, and `VectorStores` - `MissingTypes.cs` — types present in old generated output but missing from new generator output - `ModelFactoryShims.cs` — shims for `PersistentAgentsModelFactory` factory methods - `PropertyShims.cs` — property-level backward compat shims - **Renamed client builder extension**: `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` → `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` - **New generated model factory**: `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` (2372 lines added) - **API surface change**: ~970 additions and ~145 deletions in the public API listing files ## Gaps Found - The `CHANGELOG.md` unreleased section `## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` is completely empty — all four subsections (`Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, `Other Changes`) are blank despite significant changes - The metadata parameter type change (`IReadOnlyDictionary` → `IDictionary`) is a **breaking change** that is not documented - The renamed `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` (formerly `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`) is not mentioned in the changelog - The new `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` type is not documented in the changelog - The new `BackwardCompat` shims that preserve compatibility for callers using the old `IReadOnlyDictionary` metadata parameter type are not mentioned 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section to add entries for all changes introduced in the emitter migration. Suggested content: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Added `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` as the new centralized model factory for test and mock support. ### Breaking Changes - Metadata parameters across `PersistentAgentsAdministrationClient`, `ThreadMessages`, `ThreadRuns`, `Threads`, and `VectorStores` changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary`. Backward-compatible overloads accepting `IReadOnlyDictionary` are provided as `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]` shims for existing callers. - `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` was renamed to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`. ### Other Changes - Migrated package code generation to the new TypeSpec HTTP client C# emitter. No functional behavior change is expected for most users. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippets are required for this change; the CHANGELOG update is pure documentation. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections. No structural changes are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — confirm no unexpected README diffs 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — confirm the public API listing is consistent 5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24170377439/agentic_workflow) · ● 446.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57973","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T03:18:43Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T03:18:43Z"}

Suggested content: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Added `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` as the new centralized model factory for test and mock support. ### Breaking Changes - Metadata parameters across `PersistentAgentsAdministrationClient`, `ThreadMessages`, `ThreadRuns`, `Threads`, and `VectorStores` changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary`. Backward-compatible overloads accepting `IReadOnlyDictionary` are provided as `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]` shims for existing callers. - `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` was renamed to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`. ### Other Changes - Migrated package code generation to the new TypeSpec HTTP client C# emitter. No functional behavior change is expected for most users. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippets are required for this change; the CHANGELOG update is pure documentation. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections. No structural changes are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — confirm no unexpected README diffs 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — confirm the public API listing is consistent 5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24170377439/agentic_workflow) · ● 446.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57973","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T03:18:43Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T03:18:43Z"}

{"total_count":24,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4090517017,"number":57183,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization","body":"## Bug description
When using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.

The exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:
`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`

## Package and environment
- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`
- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`
- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents
- OS: Windows
- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`

## Expected behavior
After the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.

## Actual behavior
The full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.

## Reproduction notes
1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.
2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.
4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.

## Minimal observed output
```text
assistant(stream)> <full answer omitted>
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent
Unhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel<Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall>.Write(...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()
```

## Suspected root cause
The generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.

Relevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`

## Impact
- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.
- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.
- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.

## Workaround currently used
Application-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.

## Request
Please confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"brandonh-msft","id":20270743,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/brandonh-msft","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1069434707,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention","name":"Service Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent OpenTelemetryScope throws if arguments are invalid JSON","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.5

### Describe the bug

From time to time we observe an issue with FunctionCalls where the Arguments seems to contain illegal JSON that can not be deserialized, which in turn throws an Exception in the `ProcessToolCalls` function of `OpenTelemetryScope`.

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Telemetry/OpenTelemetryScope.cs#L1147

### Expected behavior

* The issue (I would say) is that there is an assumption on arguments to be valid JSON. 
* The error should not occur in the `OpenTelemetryScope`, before the user has any chance to handle it.
* Also, shouldn't agents enfore valid JSON responses? (structured outputs or something, am I doing something wrong?)

### Actual behavior

An exception when function call arguments are deserialized.

```
The JSON value could not be converted to System.String. Path: $.repeatable | LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 64.

at System.Text.Json.ThrowHelper.ReThrowWithPath(ReadStack& state, Utf8JsonReader& reader, Exception ex)
at System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonConverter`1.ReadCore(Utf8JsonReader& reader, T& value, JsonSerializerOptions options, ReadStack& state)
at System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.ReadFromSpan[TValue](ReadOnlySpan`1 json, JsonTypeInfo`1 jsonTypeInfo)
at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)
at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)
at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()
at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()
at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.AsyncStreamingUpdateCollection.AsyncStreamingUpdateEnumerator.DisposeAsyncCore()
at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.AsyncStreamingUpdateCollection.AsyncStreamingUpdateEnumerator.DisposeAsync()
at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.AsyncStreamingUpdateCollection.GetValuesFromPageAsync(ClientResult page)+MoveNext()
at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.AsyncStreamingUpdateCollection.GetValuesFromPageAsync(ClientResult page)+System.Threading.Tasks.Sources.IValueTaskSource<System.Boolean>.GetResult()
at System.ClientModel.AsyncCollectionResult`1.GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken)+MoveNext()
at System.ClientModel.AsyncCollectionResult`1.GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken)+MoveNext()
at System.ClientModel.AsyncCollectionResult`1.GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken)+MoveNext()
at System.ClientModel.AsyncCollectionResult`1.GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken)+System.Threading.Tasks.Sources.IValueTaskSource<System.Boolean>.GetResult()
at Services.AzureOpenAiClient.ProcessRunAsync(AsyncCollectionResult`1 responseStream, String userKey, String threadId, ThreadRun run, FormSnapshot activeForm, CancellationToken cancellationToken)+MoveNext()

```

### Reproduction Steps

Every time the agents calls a function but passes invalid JSON, the code in our codebase that fails looks like this:

```csharp
async IAsyncEnumerable<AssistantStreamingUpdate> ProcessRunAsync(
    AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate> responseStream, string threadId, ThreadRun? run,
    [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken)
{
    do
    {
        List<ToolOutput> toolOutputs = [];

        await foreach (var streamingUpdate in responseStream) // this throws
        {
            // we never get here
        }

        if (toolOutputs.Count > 0)
        {
            responseStream =
                _persistentAgentsClient.Runs.SubmitToolOutputsToStreamAsync(run, toolOutputs, cancellationToken);
            continue;
        }

        break;
    } while (true);
}
```

I can not wrap it inside a try catch since inside th foreach I am yielding individual parts back over the API:
```csharp
// `processUserMessageAsync` is the IAsyncEnumerable<AssistantStreamingUpdate>
await foreach (var update in processUserMessageAsync.WithCancellation(cancellationToken))
{
    await Response.WriteAsync($"{JsonSerializer.Serialize(update, options)}\n",
        cancellationToken=[REDACTED] Environment

Microsoft.Agents.AI 1.0.0-preview.260128.1

### Describe the bug

We’re encountering a JSON parsing exception when using ChatClientAgent.RunAsync with the gpt-5 model. The same code works correctly when switching to gpt-4o.

This appears to be either a service-side incompatibility with Agents or an SDK parsing issue when handling the final response block.

### Expected behavior

ChatClientAgent.RunAsync returns an AgentResponse

### Actual behavior

ChatClientAgent.RunAsync throws an exception:

`The input does not contain any JSON tokens. Expected the input to start with a valid JSON token, when isFinalBlock is true.
LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 0.
`

### Reproduction Steps

```    
    var chatMessage = new ChatMessage
    {
      Role = ChatRole.User,
      Contents = [new DataContent(image, "image/png")]
    };

    var response = await _chatClientAgent.RunAsync(chatMessage, session, cancellationToken=[REDACTED] Environment

Streaming update result in "The input does not contain any JSON tokens" exception","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

Sometimes (not always) when I iterate over `AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate>` I get an error stating:

```
The input does not contain any JSON tokens. Expected the input to start with a valid JSON token, when isFinalBlock is true. LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 0.
```

* I have the same problem with both `gpt-4.1` and `gpt-5`
* **Worth pointing out that it works if I use the same agent and prompt from the AI Foundry Agent  playground**

The error comes after `UpdateKind` = RunInProgress

```csharp
PersistentThreadMessage messageResponse = _client.Messages.CreateMessage(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    role: MessageRole.User,
    content: request.Prompt);

AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate> stream = _client.Runs.CreateRunStreamingAsync(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    agentId: _agent.Id
);

await foreach (StreamingUpdate streamingUpdate in stream) // This is where the error occurs after RunInProgress
{
      _logger.LogInformation(streamingUpdate.UpdateKind.ToString());
}
```

### Expected behavior

If this is due to a problem with the payload, such as an empty response from the LLM or a throttling error, I would expect to see this error

### Actual behavior

I assume the response from the LLM is not in an expected format and that this is an internal bug. 

### Reproduction Steps

N/A

### Environment

- MCPToolDefinition does not support the project_connection_id property like in Python MCPTool class","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

The class MCPToolDefinition has no way to set the `project_connection_id` property like we can on the [MCPTool class](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-ai-agents/azure.ai.agents.models.mcptool?view=azure-python-preview) in python.

The `project_connection_id` is a property that is necessary when creating an agent that uses a MCP tool already created in Foundry nextgen.

Here are some examples in python:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/main/sdk/ai/azure-ai-projects/samples/agents/tools/sample_agent_mcp_with_project_connection.py#L48

```python
    tool = MCPTool(
        server_label="api-specs",
        server_url="https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp",
        require_approval="always",
        project_connection_id=os.environ["MCP_PROJECT_CONNECTION_ID"],
    )
```

I believe the `project_connection_id` is metadata, in that it does not need to be in the MCPToolResource (i.e. per thread run) thus why it could be in the MCPToolDefinition.

### Expected behavior

Be able to have extra dynamic parameters that can be passed to the REST API of Foundry to align with the Foundry nextgen platform.

### Actual behavior

Impossible to set the the property in any shape or form.

### Reproduction Steps

```csharp
new MCPToolDefinition("MCP-Server-Label", "MCP-Remote-URL")
```

MCPToolDefinition does not expose the `project_connection_id` property

### Environment

OS: Windows 11 25H2 26200.6584

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-generated issue identifies missing CHANGELOG entries for `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` 1.2.0-beta.11, following a TypeSpec emitter migration in PR #57631 that introduced breaking changes and new types without documentation.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` (1.2.0-beta.11 Unreleased)
**Affected API:** `PersistentAgentsAdministrationClient`, `ThreadMessages`, `ThreadRuns`, `Threads`, `VectorStores`, `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`, `AgentsPersistentModelFactory`
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG `## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section is empty despite significant changes from the TypeSpec emitter migration
- Metadata parameter type changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary` across multiple clients — a breaking change with no documentation
- `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` renamed to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` — undocumented
- New `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` type added (~2372 lines) — not mentioned in changelog
- New `BackwardCompat` shims added under `src/Custom/BackwardCompat/` — not mentioned

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/CHANGELOG.md` to document all changes introduced by the emitter migration in the 1.2.0-beta.11 unreleased section.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue, not a code bug. The code changes from PR #57631 (commit `3e09cc59`) are already merged; the issue is that the CHANGELOG was not updated to reflect them.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Open `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/CHANGELOG.md` and confirm the `## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section is empty.
2. Review PR #57631 to confirm the full scope of API changes (`IReadOnlyDictionary` → `IDictionary`, renamed builder extensions, new model factory).
3. Update the CHANGELOG with entries for: breaking change (metadata parameter type), renamed extension class, new `AgentsPersistentModelFactory`, and migration to new TypeSpec emitter.
4. Run `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` to confirm public API listing consistency.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` is a data-plane client SDK (not management or provisioning)
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — matches service label used on reference issues #57183, #53817, #55561, #55585, #54411 for this same package
- **Confidence:** High — package is explicitly named, unambiguously maps to `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/`, reference issues confirm both labels
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — the issue's "AI Agents" service label matches this single-label entry; no more specific multi-label entry for `%AI Agents` exists in the file
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko @RaviPidaparthi (line 86)
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; @mentioned ServiceOwners via routing comment; issue not assigned (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top from line 1228. All entries in the lower half of the file relate to CodeGen, Mgmt, Provisioning, Storage, etc. — none include `%AI Agents`. First match found at line 85.
</details>
